description_long: This utility pole is constructed primarily from weathered wood, featuring a tall vertical shaft with a single horizontal crossarm near the top. The crossarm supports several ceramic insulators, and multiple metal rungs are attached vertically along the pole for climbing access. A set of electrical wires is strung between the insulators, with one wire hanging loosely below. On one side of the pole, a cylindrical electrical transformer and additional electrical hardware are mounted, connected by cables. The wood surface shows a natural grain and some signs of outdoor exposure, while the metal and ceramic components are utilitarian and functional.
description: A tall wooden utility pole with a horizontal crossarm, insulators, attached wires, and a mounted transformer.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the pole, crossarm, insulators, climbing rungs, and the transformer with attached wires.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the pole, the protruding transformer, and the attached cables.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the crossarm, insulators, climbing rungs, and the loosely hanging wire.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the pole's slim profile and the transformer hardware from a different angle.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted near the top of the pole, supporting insulators and wires.
